name: mc_early_window_excluded_from_knn_pool
description: AC-4 proof fixture (audit MEDIUM). A 70-day SPY history. The first MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1 days (the early-window days) have their rolling 20-day volatility computed on FEWER than 20 observations -- a short sample. np.std with ddof=0 is a downward-biased estimator of the true sigma and the bias grows sharply as the sample shrinks (n=2 -> ~20% low, n=5 -> ~6% low), and spy_vols[0] is hard-set to 0.0. today's query point always uses a full 20-observation window, so the bias is one-sided: early-window days look artificially calmer than reality. In this fixture the early-window days (indices 0..MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS-2) have SPY returns tightly hugging today's return, so a kNN that still admits them as candidates selects them; the later, full-window days have SPY returns farther from today's. The holding ticker AAA GAINS on the early-window days (daily_ret +0.10) and LOSES on every full-window day (daily_ret -0.10). AC-4 excludes the first MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1 short-sample days from the kNN candidate pool. With them excluded the only candidates are full-window loss days -- the bootstrap distribution flips sign and prob_beating moves from the structural value 100.0 (early-window days in the pool, holding gains) to the structural value 0.0 (early-window days excluded, holding loses).
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history. The early-window day count is derived from math_engine.MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS at test time, not hardcoded. The regime selection is observable through prob_beating because the holding's early-vs-late returns have opposite sign.

early_window_in_pool.meaning: kNN admitted the short-sample early-window days; holding gains there; bootstrap all +10.0%
early_window_in_pool.structural_prob_reason: current_symphony_return 0.0 is strictly below every bootstrapped path (+10.0%); searchsorted below_count=0; prob = 100.0. A deterministic boundary result, not a random draw.

early_window_excluded.meaning: kNN candidate pool excludes the first MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS-1 days; only full-window loss days remain; bootstrap all -10.0%
early_window_excluded.structural_prob_reason: current_symphony_return 0.0 is strictly above every bootstrapped path (-10.0%); searchsorted below_count=paths; prob = 0.0. A deterministic boundary result, not a random draw.

derivation: Verified by replicating run_monte_carlo's internal kNN. Pre-fix (early-window days admitted) the 8 nearest days are entirely indices < MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS-1 for k in {5,8,12} (their returns hug today's). Post-fix (first MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS-1 days dropped from the candidate pool) the 8 nearest days are entirely full-window indices, regardless of whether the distance is standardized. The holding's opposite-sign per-region returns turn that into an observable prob_beating: pre-fix 100.0, post-fix 0.0.
